paper_title: Cerro Ballena Fossil Documentation and Analysis

methods.description: Process of excavating and collecting marine mammal fossils from the Cerro Ballena site, revealed during road expansion along the Pan-American Highway in the Atacama Region of Chile.

steps.description: From 2010 to 2012, road expansion along the Pan-American Highway in Atacama Region of Chile opened a 20 × 250 m quarry at a site, called Cerro Ballena, which revealed over 40 complete and partial marine mammal skeletons.
steps.searchable_summary: excavate and collect marine mammal fossils from Cerro Ballena site

methods.description: Capturing, processing, and rendering three-dimensional digital datasets of in situ skeletal remains using photogrammetry and computer vision tools.

steps.description: Photogrammetry and computer vision datasets for fossil rorquals were captured with 20 and 30 cm aluminium scale bars and metal markers on a Canon 5D with multiple lenses, and geotagged using a Garmin Etrex GPS.
steps.searchable_summary: capture 3D datasets using photogrammetry and geotagging

steps.description: Raw digital datasets were processed into coherent models by aligning datasets, cleaning up noise and removing redundant data using GEOMAGIC.
steps.searchable_summary: process raw datasets into 3D models using GEOMAGIC
